slides.lead: Demonstration deck for the template. All text is sample (dummy) content and exists only to show off the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Put the event name, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.
Team slide. Uses colored initials instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Traction, retention and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: A great product nobody can find still loses. Value compounds when reach and retention meet.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: big heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or chapter.

items: month over month growth in active accounts
net revenue retention across the base
logo churn after the new onboarding
bullets: Measure outcomes, not vanity: retention beats raw signups.
Start where the funnel already leaks the most value.
A small cohort with a clear metric beats a big launch without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: Each active user creates the next one.
bullets: New users land through a clear, low-friction entry.
They hit the first value moment fast.
A habit forms before the trial ends.
Happy users invite the next cohort.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales to fill.

slides.lead: Same product, two ways to reach the market.
cards: Users sign up and pay alone. Cheap to scale, thin per deal.
Reps close larger accounts. Higher cost, bigger contracts.
Free usage warms leads before a rep ever calls.
Self-serve for small teams, sales for enterprise.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six common building blocks of a SaaS product.
cards: Get users to first value in minutes, not days.
Live view of usage, funnels and drop-off.
Plans, upgrades and dunning that just work.
Plug into the tools customers already use.
SOC 2, SSO and data that stays in your control.
A team that earns trust and reduces churn.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Acquisition slows, so the focus shifts to retention and expansion.
A loop catches and each cohort pulls in the next, faster.
Expansion revenue compounds and the base funds itself.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a short caption.

slides.quote: The startups that win are not the ones with the most features, but the ones that turn users into habits the fastest.
slides.by: Sample quote to show off the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget under pressure.
bullets: Numbers tell the story before the pitch does.
You always know how many months of cash remain.
The investor outlasts most of your features.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A leaky bucket no amount of spend can fill.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one big sentence centered on the page. Use it for the key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opens the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch into the product and build a live cohort view.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and call to action.
